rationale.R1.3: The section contains a serious internal contradiction between the metric actually reported (ROC-AUC) and the property the authors claim to be measuring (early enrichment).
rationale.RX.1: The text does not provide clear evidence for uncertainty disclosure, but it does mention that ROC-AUC is insensitive to performance at the very top of the list.
rationale.RX.3: The section mostly stays within scope with one drift, as it discusses virtual screening and ranking quality, but it also mentions analog bias in DUD-E.
rationale.RX.4: The output introduces new errors, fabrications, or harmful content by overstating the significance of the results and making unsubstantiated claims about the mechanism behind the improvement.
rationale.RX.5: The section does not provide clear provenance and traceability for the methods used, as it does not mention how DUD-E actives/decoys were used, whether any filtering/clustering was applied, or how ties in scores are broken.
